title: Nematoceras papillosum
intro: Nematoceras papillosum, also known as the 'Pill Bearing Fungus', is a type of fungus that belongs to the genus Nematoceras. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Nematoceras papillosum include polysaccharides, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas and infusions, as well as dried and powdered extracts, which are used to relieve respiratory symptoms and support immune function.
taxonomy: Nematoceras papillosum is classified within the family Nematoceratidae, which is a group of extinct ammonites. These ammonites are part of the subclass Ammonoidea, a subgroup of cephalopod mollusks. The subclass Ammonoidea is further classified within the class Cephalopoda. Cephalopods are a diverse group of marine animals that also include modern species like squid, octopuses, and nautiluses. The classification of Nematoceras papillosum reflects its evolutionary relationships within the cephalopod lineage.
uses: Nematoceras papillosum is a genus of extinct ammonites.
